sections.paragraph:
τηλαυγῶς adv. of τηλαυγής (Strabo 17, 1, 30; POxy 886, 24 [III a.d.]; Philo, Congr. 24, 25; Cleopatra p. 15, 38) of a visual image (very) plainly/clearly Mk 8:25 (v.l. δηλαυγῶς, q.v.—B-D-F §119, 4; Rdm.2 37; s. Mlt-H. 283).—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Cleopatra Cleopatra = conversations alleged between a queen Cleopatra and philosophers (alchemists), date uncertain—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rdm. Rdm. = LRadermacher, Neutestamentliche Grammatik—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1001
